evals.prompt: I just downloaded my Banco Popular joint account statement for March 2026 and saved it as Banco-Popular-Joint-2026-03.csv in /Statements/Personal/Joint/Banks/. Can you process it and organize it?
evals.expected_output: The skill detects the Banco Popular format, learns/applies the format mapping, parses all transactions, logs the results to statement-processing-YYYY-MM-DD.log, and confirms the file is organized correctly in the folder structure.

assertions.name: Bank detection accuracy
assertions.description: Skill correctly identifies the file as Banco Popular statement

assertions.name: Format mapping created
assertions.description: Format mapping for Banco Popular is created and stored in .statement-formats.json

assertions.name: Transaction parsing success
assertions.description: All transactions in the statement are successfully parsed (100% success rate)

assertions.name: Logging functionality
assertions.description: Processing log is created with timestamps and transaction details

assertions.name: Ease of use vs baseline
assertions.description: With-skill solution requires fewer manual steps and less code than without-skill baseline

evals.prompt: I have a Dart Bank statement for March 2026 with all my Portal42 client payments. It's at /Statements/Business-You/Banks/Dart-Bank-2026-03.csv. Please process it, match transactions to unpaid FreshBooks invoices, and give me a report of what was marked paid vs. what needs review.
evals.expected_output: The skill parses the Dart Bank statement, matches transactions to unpaid Portal42 invoices by amount + dispensary name, generates a detailed CSV report showing: (a) matched transactions auto-marked paid in FreshBooks, (b) ambiguous matches needing manual review, (c) unmatched transactions. Logs show FreshBooks API calls.

assertions.name: FreshBooks matching algorithm
assertions.description: Skill implements amount + dispensary name matching logic as specified

assertions.name: Three-category report generation
assertions.description: Report correctly categorizes matches into: matched (auto-marked), ambiguous (needs review), unmatched

assertions.name: High-confidence match accuracy
assertions.description: Matched transactions have ≥90% confidence; ambiguous have 70-89% confidence

assertions.name: Actionable recommendations
assertions.description: Report includes clear guidance on which transactions need manual review vs. auto-marked as paid

assertions.name: Automation time savings
assertions.description: With-skill implementation saves significant time vs. manual FreshBooks reconciliation

evals.prompt: I have a new bank account with a bank I haven't used before. The statement is at /Statements/Personal/You/Banks/NewBank-March-2026.csv. Can you learn its format and add it to the mapping so future statements are automatically parsed?
evals.expected_output: The skill reads the statement, auto-detects the column headers (date, amount, merchant), creates a format mapping, stores it in .statement-formats.json, and confirms the format is learned. Future statements from this bank are parsed automatically.

assertions.name: Format auto-detection success
assertions.description: Skill successfully identifies key columns (date, amount, merchant) without manual input

assertions.name: Format mapping persistence
assertions.description: Format mapping is saved to .statement-formats.json with >0.8 confidence score

assertions.name: Zero future manual effort
assertions.description: Future NewBank statements parse automatically using learned format (no relearning needed)

assertions.name: High parsing accuracy
assertions.description: All transactions from the statement are correctly parsed (100% success rate)

assertions.name: Learn-once, use-forever approach
assertions.description: Skill demonstrates one-time learning pattern that eliminates repetitive format setup

evals.prompt: I have three credit card statements: AmEx business card, Chase personal card, and a Visa. They're in /Statements/Personal/You/Credit_Cards/ and /Statements/Business-You/Credit_Cards/. Can you process all of them and learn their formats? I want to make sure they're organized correctly.
evals.expected_output: The skill processes all three credit card statements, detects each format (AmEx, Chase, Visa), learns and stores the mappings for each, organizes them in the correct folders, and logs all three statements as processed. Format mappings show credit card-specific columns (cardholder, transaction type, etc.).

assertions.name: Multi-format simultaneous learning
assertions.description: Skill correctly learns and distinguishes between AmEx, Chase, and Visa formats in one operation

assertions.name: Credit card-specific column detection
assertions.description: Mappings capture credit card-specific fields (cardholder, transaction type, category, rewards)

assertions.name: Batch processing capability
assertions.description: Skill efficiently processes all three statements without requiring separate runs

assertions.name: Correct folder organization
assertions.description: Statements are organized into correct folders (Personal/You/Credit_Cards and Business-You/Credit_Cards)

assertions.name: 100% transaction parsing
assertions.description: All 27 transactions across three cards are successfully parsed

evals.prompt: I processed my March Dart Bank statement and got a report. Can you flag any transactions that look unusual or anomalous compared to my typical spending patterns? For example, unusually large amounts, transactions at unusual times, or vendors I don't normally use. I want to catch fraud early.
evals.expected_output: The skill analyzes the Dart Bank transactions, identifies anomalies based on amount deviations, merchant patterns, and frequency, generates a flagged transactions list showing: (a) transactions significantly larger than average, (b) new/unusual merchants, (c) potential duplicates or suspicious patterns. Report is saved to a separate 'anomalies-YYYY-MM-DD.csv' for review.

assertions.name: Amount deviation detection
assertions.description: Skill identifies transactions significantly larger than typical spend (e.g., >2.5 standard deviations)

assertions.name: Merchant pattern analysis
assertions.description: Skill detects new/unusual merchants and suspicious keywords (crypto, gambling, wire transfers)

assertions.name: Duplicate detection
assertions.description: Skill identifies potential duplicate transactions (same amount, merchant, date)

assertions.name: Severity classification
assertions.description: Flagged transactions are classified by severity (HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) based on anomaly strength

assertions.name: Fraud detection effectiveness
assertions.description: Skill correctly identifies 95%+ of fraud patterns with <8% false positive rate
